facts.text: Punta Gorda was founded in 1884 when Isaac Trabue’s instructions led surveyor Kelly Harvey to subdivide the area, filing the plan in Manatee County on February 24, 1885.
The city incorporated on December 7, 1887 after the railroad arrived and the Hotel Punta Gorda opened, sparking a boom in winter visitors, agriculture, and fishing.
Punta Gorda’s public library, the oldest branch in the Charlotte County system, opened in 1908 inside an Episcopal church rectory before moving to a Masonic lodge in 1928.
In 2004, Category 4 Hurricane Charley swept through Punta Gorda, damaging many historic buildings but also sparking a revitalization of the downtown waterfront.
The Blanchard House Museum, still standing today, offers visitors a glimpse into middle‑class African‑American life in early Punta Gorda.
